rider_metrics.name: Overall Rider Score
rider_metrics.note: Composite of posture, balance, symmetry, stability, and leg position.

rider_metrics.name: Rider Posture
rider_metrics.note: Derived from torso alignment, head offset, and shoulder level variance.

rider_metrics.name: Rider Balance
rider_metrics.note: Uses seat offset, drift, bounce, and upper-body control.

rider_metrics.name: Rider Symmetry
rider_metrics.note: Uses left/right shoulder, hip, knee, and ankle balance.

rider_metrics.name: Rider Stability
rider_metrics.note: Uses torso variability, vertical motion, and rocking.

rider_metrics.name: Rider Leg Position
rider_metrics.note: Uses knee/ankle angles and lower-leg steadiness.

rider_metrics.name: Torso Angle
rider_metrics.note: Lower values mean a more vertical torso.

rider_metrics.name: Head Alignment
rider_metrics.note: Horizontal offset between the head and shoulder line.

rider_metrics.name: Seat Offset
rider_metrics.note: Horizontal offset from the saddle midpoint.

rider_metrics.name: Shoulder Level Variance
rider_metrics.note: How level the rider shoulders stay through time.

rider_metrics.name: Hip Level Variance
rider_metrics.note: How level the rider hips stay through time.

horse_metrics.name: Horse Movement Score
horse_metrics.note: Composite of cadence, drift, rock amplitude, and motion consistency.

horse_metrics.name: Horse Rhythm
horse_metrics.note: Rhythm compared with the expected range for the selected discipline.

horse_metrics.name: Horse Consistency
horse_metrics.note: Measures repeatability of motion and topline stability.

horse_metrics.name: Horse Symmetry
horse_metrics.note: Side-view proxy for left/right consistency and topline evenness.

horse_metrics.name: Drift X
horse_metrics.note: Horizontal saddle drift across the ride.

saddle_metrics.name: Saddle Stability
saddle_metrics.note: Overall saddle motion, rocking, and drift stability.

saddle_metrics.name: Rider-Saddle Balance
saddle_metrics.note: Relationship between rider position and saddle movement.

discipline_metrics.name: Symmetry
discipline_metrics.note: Balanced seat, steady contact, and clean straightness matter most.

recommendations: Check withers clearance and front balance. Use fitter guidance if the saddle appears to tip forward.
Check panel contact and girthing. Excessive rock can indicate bridging or unstable support.
Review girth placement, pad grip, and saddle alignment to reduce movement.
Use core engagement and a softer follow-through to reduce bounce.
Evaluate panel contact and consider a fitter review if bridging persists.
Cadence was not detected cleanly; use a longer, steadier straight-line clip for rhythm analysis.
Reduce upper-body motion and keep the torso quieter through the stride.
Maintain a steadier lower leg and keep the heel quietly under the hip.
Use a more rhythmic straight-line clip if you want clearer cadence analysis.
Review saddle placement and withers clearance for a more centered position.
Balanced seat, steady contact, and clean straightness matter most.

missing_data: Rider joint angles could not be measured reliably.
Cadence could not be inferred from the available motion.

disclaimer: This analysis provides visual and movement-based indicators and is not a substitute for an in-person assessment by a qualified saddle fitter, veterinarian, physiotherapist, or equine professional.
discipline_notes: Balanced seat, steady contact, and clean straightness matter most.

Stability.improve: Reduce rocking and drift to raise stability.

Alignment.improve: Balance saddle to match topline and keep even contact.

Symmetry.improve: Level shoulders/hips and equalize stirrup pressure.

Rhythm.improve: Keep steady tempo; use metronome or poles if needed.

Clearance.improve: Maintain withers clearance; adjust pad/tree if low.

Tracking.improve: Film steady side view for higher tracking confidence.

Bounce.improve: Engage core and follow motion to reduce bounce.

Drift.improve: Check girth, pad grip, and straightness to limit drift.

saddle.note: Limited: side-view roll estimation is approximate.

horse.note: Side-view only; lateral symmetry not observed.

notes: Pose landmarks were not reliable enough for rider joint analysis.
Measurements that depend on rider posture will be estimated from the tracked saddle motion only.

flags: Higher saddle rocking detected across the ride.
Higher vertical bounce detected at the saddle midpoint.
Rear/front clearance variation suggests bridging tendency.

to_improve: Reduce upper-body motion and keep the torso quieter through the stride.
Maintain a steadier lower leg and keep the heel quietly under the hip.
Use a more rhythmic straight-line clip if you want clearer cadence analysis.
Review saddle placement and withers clearance for a more centered position.
drills: Ride straight lines and check that shoulders stay level through the stride.
Use light two-point or half-seat work to quiet the lower leg and reduce bounce.
Repeat short intervals and compare the same metrics across sessions for trend tracking.

notes: Pose landmarks unavailable; gear auto-detect limited.

analysis_sections_html:
Executive Summary

Overall score 13 with rider score 13. Horse movement scored 0 and saddle stability scored 0. Discipline focus: General Riding.

Horse Movement

Cadence + motion consistency
Saddle Stability
0

Rocking + drift + balance
Symmetry
66
Needs Attention
Rider left/right alignment
Video

horse riding.mp4

Frames analyzed: 256 | Confidence: 11.3%

Discipline context

Balanced seat, steady contact, and clean straightness matter most.

Visual Evidence

Annotated representative frame with detected saddle and rider reference points.

Motion trend summary from the extracted tracking series.

Key Strengths

No strong areas were isolated with enough confidence.

Areas for Improvement

  • Overall analysis: 13/100
  • Rider posture: 0/100
  • Rider balance: 0/100
  • Rider stability: 0/100
  • Rider leg position: 0/100
  • Horse movement: 0/100
  • Horse rhythm: 20/100
  • Horse consistency: 0/100
  • Horse symmetry: 0/100
  • Saddle stability: 0/100
  • Saddle position: 0/100
  • Rider-saddle balance: 0/100

Recommendations

  • Check withers clearance and front balance. Use fitter guidance if the saddle appears to tip forward.
  • Check panel contact and girthing. Excessive rock can indicate bridging or unstable support.
  • Review girth placement, pad grip, and saddle alignment to reduce movement.
  • Use core engagement and a softer follow-through to reduce bounce.
  • Evaluate panel contact and consider a fitter review if bridging persists.
  • Cadence was not detected cleanly; use a longer, steadier straight-line clip for rhythm analysis.
  • Rider level estimate: Beginner (13/100).
  • Reduce upper-body motion and keep the torso quieter through the stride.
  • Maintain a steadier lower leg and keep the heel quietly under the hip.
  • Use a more rhythmic straight-line clip if you want clearer cadence analysis.
  • Review saddle placement and withers clearance for a more centered position.
  • Balanced seat, steady contact, and clean straightness matter most.

Data Quality / Confidence

Tracking success20.6%
Pose confidence0.0%
Analysis confidence11.3%
Pose samples0
Frames analyzed256
Missing dataRider joint angles could not be measured reliably.; Cadence could not be inferred from the available motion.

Professional Disclaimer

This analysis provides visual and movement-based indicators and is not a substitute for an in-person assessment by a qualified saddle fitter, veterinarian, physiotherapist, or equine professional.
